tool.name: infoalign
tool.summary: Report row counts, column counts, and per-row gap statistics for an alignment

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `infoalign` reports stable alignment metadata rather than rewriting the alignment itself. The current Epithema implementation summarizes one aligned FASTA or Stockholm input as a governed table containing the overall row and column counts plus per-row ungapped-length and gap-count statistics.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts exactly one local aligned FASTA or Stockholm alignment path and preserves the original row ordering in the reported table.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: CLI and service output is a deterministic table report. The current row schema includes the alignment identifier when available, the 1-based row ordinal, the row identifier, the pairwise-versus-multiple classification, the ungapped residue count, and the gap count for each alignment row.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema infoalign`. Current Rust service coverage exercises the committed three-row Stockholm fixture and locks down the expected row count, column count, and row-level gap statistics.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release reports a conservative metadata subset only. It does not attempt broader historical EMBOSS summary fields, scoring annotations, or sequence-derived biological interpretation beyond the typed alignment statistics.

artifacts.label: Multiple-alignment Stockholm fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed Stockholm alignment fixture used to validate stable row-order metadata reporting.

examples.title: Summarize a three-row Stockholm alignment
examples.description: Reports stable per-row gap and ungapped-length statistics for the committed multiple-alignment fixture.

expected_outputs.label: Alignment summary table
expected_outputs.description: A deterministic per-row table reporting ordinals, identifiers, ungapped lengths, and gap counts.
